mnemonics: EXIT Ends In Termination (reminds you that exit() terminates the calling process)
keyPoints: The exit() function terminates a process and returns an exit status to the parent.
It flushes all I/O buffers and closes all open file descriptors before termination.
The exit status is an integer, where 0 typically indicates success and non-zero indicates an error.
commonMistakes: Confusing exit() with _exit() or _Exit(), which do not flush I/O buffers.
Forgetting that exit() does not return to the calling function; it ends the entire process.
Using arbitrary numbers for exit status instead of standard codes like EXIT_SUCCESS or EXIT_FAILURE.
